title: Disease-specific walkthrough set (e.g. align with Ewing track)
context: The general walkthroughs in M0/M2 use papers selected for methodological diversity. A disease-specific set would use papers from a single disease area (e.g. Ewing sarcoma, to align with the Elyos Ewing track), giving advocates whose organisations focus on that cancer a more directly relevant set of worked examples. The disease scope is an open question (PLAN.md Open Q5) and this task is unscheduled pending a scope decision and confirmed advocacy partner with that disease focus.
objective: Author a set of annotated walkthroughs using licence-verified, fully open-access papers from a specific oncology disease area (disease TBD pending scope decision), applying the matching worksheets inline and obtaining dual high-tier sign-off for each walkthrough.
acceptanceCriteria: Disease scope decision (PLAN.md Open Q5) is resolved and the target disease area is confirmed by the maintainer before authoring begins.

At least three walkthroughs using licence-verified, fully open-access papers (CC0, CC-BY, or CC-BY-SA) from the confirmed disease area, each recorded in data/sources.yaml with PMCID, DOI, licence, licenceVerifiedDate, and verifiedBy.
Each walkthrough covers a different study type (e.g. RCT, observational, systematic review) to span methodological diversity within the disease context.
Each walkthrough applies the matching worksheet inline and renders no verdicts on findings or treatments.
Carries a persistent 'education only — not medical advice' notice; passes the advice-language checklist.
Any reviewer with a material COI on a paper used recuses from that walkthrough's sign-off.
Passes the licence + provenance build gate and CI.
Each walkthrough signed off by a credentialed oncologist with relevant disease expertise (recorded in the review ledger).
Each walkthrough signed off by an experienced patient advocate familiar with the disease community (recorded in the review ledger).
Each walkthrough signed off by a methodology reviewer.
resources: PLAN.md (§ Open questions — Q5 disease scope; § Dependencies — PMC OA Subset)
TASKS.md (Backlog / future — depends on disease-scope decision Q5)
output: A set of disease-specific annotated walkthrough documents (content/walkthroughs/disease-specific/) committed to the repo, with sources.yaml entries for all papers, CI-green, and carrying dual high-tier sign-off plus methodology review for each walkthrough.
